articles.title: Dollar Rallies on a Hot US PPI Report
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) rallied to a 1.5-week high on Wednesday, finishing up by 0.22%. The dollar moved higher on Wednesday on the stronger-than-expected US Apr PPI report, which was hawkish for Fed policy. The dollar also has safe-haven support on concerns that the US-Iran ceasefire may break down...

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Correction could extend to 0.7200
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar’s uptrend seems to have met some decent resistance in the 0.7270-0.7280 band so far, with AUD/USD still looking for a strong catalyst to extend its rally and dispute the yearly peaks and higher levels.

articles.title: Swiss Franc dips as strong US data, hawkish Fed commentary support Dollar
articles.expand: USD/CHF advances toward 0.7830 on Thursday, gaining 0.15% on the day at the time of writing, supported by renewed demand for the US Dollar (USD) following a series of solid economic releases in the United States (US).

articles.title: UK GDP Q1 2026: Economy Grows But the Best Is Already Behind It
articles.expand: The UK economy grew 0.6% in Q1 2026, matching forecasts and marking its fastest growth in a year — but analysts warn the best may already be behind it.

articles.title: BOE Pill. The 2nd round effects of inflation may be stronger.
articles.expand: The BOE's Pill is speaking and says: The 2nd round effects on inflation may be stronger They should be focused on attacking those 2nd round effects on inflation We must not allow ourselves to drift off into deep space of unmoored inflation dynamics They do not expect 2nd round effects to be as deep as 2022. Disinflation stalled before the Iran conf...

articles.title: Gold struggles for direction below $4,700 amid stronger US Dollar and hawkish Fed outlook
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) trades under modest pressure on Thursday as the US Dollar (USD) extends its advance amid uncertainty surrounding US-Iran negotiations and a hawkish Federal Reserve (Fed) outlook.

articles.title: US: Higher inflation risks delay Fed easing – UOB
articles.expand: UOB’s Senior Economist Alvin Liew notes that United States (US) inflation pressures have broadened beyond energy, with April Consumer Price Index (CPI) and Producer Price Index (PPI) both reaccelerating and core measures drifting further above the Federal Reserve’s 2% target.

articles.title: British Pound: Political uncertainty offsets growth support – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Lee Hardman explains that the Pound (GBP) is softer despite stronger-than-expected United Kingdom (UK) Gross Domestic Product (GDP), as markets anticipate slower growth later in the year due to the energy price shock.

articles.title: KC Fed Pres Schmid: Inflation remains too high.
articles.expand: KC Fed Pres Schmid is speaking and says: Continued inflation is the most pressing risk to the economy It is clear inflation remains too high US economy has shown “remarkable resilience” and economic fundamentals remain sound Higher oil prices still drain household spending power and raise business costs US economy less vulnerable to global oil disr...

articles.expand: The USDCAD is breaking above its 100-day moving average at 1.3718, a key technical level that has repeatedly capped rallies over the past month. Recall that on Tuesday the pair briefly moved above that moving average, only to quickly reverse lower. Yesterday, sellers once again leaned against the same level and stalled the advance. articles.title: Japanese Yen steadies as strong US Dollar offsets hawkish BoJ signals
articles.expand: USD/JPY trades around 157.95 on Thursday at the time of writing, up modestly by 0.05% on the day, as the pair remains close to its highest level in two weeks.

articles.title: Japanese Yen: Higher yields support prospects for BoJ hike – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Lee Hardman notes that rising long-term JGB yields, driven by inflation risks linked to the Middle East conflict and hawkish BoJ commentary, are reinforcing expectations for a near-term policy rate increase.

articles.title: NZD/USD Signal: Can the Kiwi Hold 0.59 Support Before US Non-Farm Payrolls? - 14 May 2026
articles.expand: NZD/USD is consolidating near 0.5950, with 0.59 as a potential buy zone and 0.60 as the key upside resistance ahead of US jobs data.

The euro is holding above $1.17 and sterling is holding above $1.35, but the market does not appear done probing these support areas. articles.expand: UK economic growth held up better than expected in the first quarter of 2026, with GDP expanding 0.6% qoq in line with forecasts as stronger services activity helped offset softer industrial momentum. articles.expand: SINGAPORE: The dollar got a lift from elevated U.S. Treasury yields on Thursday as investors wagered the Federal Reserve would hike rates this year, while global focus moved to a two-day summit between U.S. President Donald Trump and China’s Xi Jinping. articles.title: Commonwealth Bank Shares Climb to $155.64 on Strong Earnings Outlook and Rate Cut Optimism
articles.expand: Commonwealth Bank of Australia shares rose 1.28% as investors responded positively to its strong performance and potential Reserve Bank of Australia rate cuts, boosting confidence in the financial sector.

articles.title: UK GDP expected to accelerate in Q1, with March data casting doubts on near-term outlook
articles.expand: The United Kingdom’s (UK) Office for National Statistics will release the preliminary estimate of the first quarter’s Gross Domestic Product (GDP) report on Thursday. Market analysts have anticipated a 0.6% growth in the three months to March, after a meager 0.1% advance in the last quarter of 2025.

articles.title: Centre may raise fuel prices if Middle East crisis continues, says RBI Governor
articles.expand: RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra warned that India may have to increase petrol and diesel prices if the ongoing Middle East conflict continues for a prolonged period. The post Centre may raise fuel prices if Middle East crisis continues, says RBI Governor appeared first on APN News .

articles.expand: Japan's government is considering a supplementary budget to ease household fuel costs ahead of summer, Kyodo reports, sending 30- and 40-year JGB yields higher on increased debt issuance fears. Speaking in London, Lane laid out the intellectual case for a likely June rate hike while acknowledging the Eurozone economy ...

articles.title: USD/JPY Continues to Climb Amid External and Domestic Pressures
articles.expand: By Analytical Department RoboForex USD/JPY rose to 157.65 on Wednesday, marking a third consecutive day of gains. articles.title: ECBs Kocher: A June hike is not a baseline for the ECB
articles.expand: ECBs Kocher on the wires saying: A June hike is not a baseline for the ECB Says that the ECB decides on a meeting by meeting basis. A 50 bps hike is very unlikely The market is for a series of hikes going into year-end. This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.

articles.title: Eurozone GDP Expands Just 0.1% in Q1, Annual Growth Slows to 0.8%
articles.expand: Eurozone economic growth slowed further in the first quarter of 2026 as higher energy costs, weak industrial activity, and lingering geopolitical uncertainty continued weighing on momentum. articles.expand: With the Fed frozen by inflation and the BOJ getting serious about rate hikes, USD/JPY is caught in the middle of a policy tug-of-war.

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Extra gains still on the cards
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar continues its march north almost unabated, lifting AUD/USD to the area of multi-year tops. For now, the pair’s positive outlook remains unchanged, propped up by elevated inflation in Oz and the RBA’s hawkish stance.
